reviews.review_text: The location is noting short of spectacular and the caravan park itself is great. ||However, we stayed in a standard cabin and it was unbelievably overpriced for what it was. They are beyond dingy and extremely old. The wood paneling in the bathroom was swollen from water damage. You can’t open the shower door without banging into the toilet. The TV was broken and the WIFI doesn’t reach that part of the park. All that for $200 a night.  Reception were lovely and did offer to send someone to take a look at the TV but for 2 nights we didn’t bother. Apparently these cabins are being decommissioned soon, fair enough, I just feel that charging $200 a night for what they are is just greedy. ||Otherwise, the other cabins around the park looked lovely from the outside. Just avoid the standard ones.

We stayed this week at Seal Rocks for the first time, and i get the hype! This location is absolutely stunning. I think one of the best beaches in Oz.
The service by the Reflections staff was warm, friendly, and super accommodating of our requests.
Being that little bit further from Sydney meant the crowd of fellow campers was friendly, with less riff raff. Lots of multi generational groups, and everyone just enjoying the pristine beaches, views, and glorious autumn sunshine.
We've already rebooked for next year.
Thanks again!!! Truly a memorable stay. There were a few tears on departure. :)
Great natural surroundings
We had a good time here. Very quiet. Most fellow campers/travellers were very friendly.
Amazing Campground. Must visit. Amazing location and very well maintained. Very friendly staff.
Amazing location; we had a beautiful kid-free weekend

We started going to Seal Rocks 5 years ago and now it’s our go to place. We go every year with our family and couldn’t think of a better place to go. This year the toilets have had a makeover and are great. The staff are always amazing and the location is spot on. We look forward to our annual trip
Must visit place staff, beach and views are great will not be disapointed.

We always love our time at Seal Rocks, it is a magical location along with Katie and Dans hospitality as park managers makes it the perfect destination for a family vacation to remember and look forward to coming back again 👍🏼

We had such a fantastic experience at Seals Rocks Caravan Park. While our original onsite Jayco caravan wasn’t great, the way the team handled it was absolutely outstanding. They upgraded us to a glamping tent and genuinely went above and beyond to make sure we were looked after.||That upgrade completely changed our stay and turned it into a truly memorable experience , we’re so grateful for the care and service we received.||The park itself is in a beautiful location, and you can really see how much effort has gone into the recent upgrades. It’s already a great park, and it’s only going to get better again once the vans are replaced with tiny homes.||We’ll definitely be back next year. Thank you again to the team for such excellent service and for making our stay so special.

We had such a fantastic experience at Seals Rocks Caravan Park. While our original onsite Jayco caravan wasn’t great, the way the team handled it was absolutely outstanding. They upgraded us to a glamping tent and genuinely went above and beyond to make sure we were looked after.

That upgrade completely changed our stay and turned it into a truly memorable experience , we’re so grateful for the care and service we received.

The park itself is in a beautiful location, and you can really see how much effort has gone into the recent upgrades. It’s already a great park, and it’s only going to get better again once the vans are replaced with tiny homes.

We’ll definitely be back next year. Thank you again to the team for such excellent service and for making our stay so special.
Caravan park is located on the mid north coast of NSW. We'll maintained grounds with immaculate facilities.

Face à une superbe plage, peu fréquentée, hormis par les occupants du lieu...
Deux autres très calmes aussi à proximité,  pour le changement...
Les emplacements sont corrects, idem pour les locaux. Tarifs dans la moyenne...
Assez loin de tout, faites vos provisions pour le séjour !!!
Une très bonne expérience (camping car) !

Do not stay in the Jayco caravans at Seal Rocks as they are nothing like they advertise on their website. Blinds are missing, oven and grill don't work, vans have no awnings, bunk bed windows are taped shut from the outside and they charge an absolute fortune to stay in them.  Reflections Seal Rocks you should be ashamed of yourselves.

Reflections Seal Rocks is set on a lovely beach, which is really for a swim. I stayed there with my campervan, and while the caravan spots are spacious, they do feel quite close together, so it’s definitely a busier park. The place has a fantastic family vibe, with lots of day visitors including young kids and babies, making it lively and cheerful. The staff were great , friendly, welcoming, and very helpful throughout our stay. It’s a great spot if you’re after a fun family holiday atmosphere. However, if you’re looking for some quiet and peaceful time, you might want to consider other parks for a more tranquil experience.
The views to have dinner was great, very friendly staff too 🙏🏽 amenities  clean

Great spot. The staff were friendly and helpful. Everything was clean 5/5.

0/5 for the parents who allow their children to run around the park on scooters screaming to each other before 6 am. They also allowed their children to throw around the outdoor chess pieces and break them. The lack of consideration for others was surprising.
Beautiful park staff very accommodating even in peak time,  everything is clean and tidy.  Just a lil walk to the beach ⛱️ 😎 which is beautiful
Beautiful view, amenities updated and clean.

The location is wonderful right near the beach. The amenities are pretty great, lots of kitchen space and the family bathroom is a great idea. However there were a few very disappointing things that impacted our experience. For context we paid almost $500 for four nights at a powered camp site. The gates were very unreliable, our codes never worked which was very painful. The grass, including our site, was full of bindies which is especially unpleasant for kids. The amenities were not kept clean enough, only being done once a day isn't enough when the park is completely full. The building with the kitchen and TV was closed at random times at night, with no obvious signage to indicate this. This was also done really loudly, with slamming doors and windows, which wasn't great with sleeping kids. The "cleaning" of that space was done at peak morning times, and seemed to consist mostly of hosing things down. The sink drains were clogged and not cleared. Overall pretty disappointing for the price.
Went tent/camping, very quiet spot, peaceful. Half hour plus drive for groceries, WiFi  not good, phone service bad

We were quite disappointed with our stay, especially considering we paid $3,000 for 6 nights in Cabin #18. The cabin was located right next to a rundown 'shack' that blocked our view. Even worse, the smell of their weed constantly drifted over to our balcony, which was very inappropriate with kids around.

The cabin itself had several broken features and felt generally worn down. While the location was convenient and the amenities were decent, the overall experience just didn’t match the price we paid.

We’re both still buzzing from our amazing stay at seal rocks reflections. On a last minute weekend getaway, the park managed to give us a sense of serenity and peacefulness to recharge batteries ready for reality. Upon arrival, our first day and night was met with heavy rains and winds but Meg in the front office brought the sunshine with her warm welcome and helpfulness. The weather luckily turned in the following days, allowing us to surf, snorkel and camp comfortably. Other than the beautiful location and friendly staff, the thing that stood out to me was the ammenities, bathrooms were constantly clean, lovely renovated and the idea of a whole area for swimmers and wetsuits to be hung up and dried shows that the park clearly knows their clientele. Thank you !!

Lovely area with great facilities.|The Cabin was massively overpriced for what you get. The Campsites where also overpriced.|When the shower screen glass hits the toilet bowl its a bit to small.|Moderate NE wind was blowing which prompted Reflections to cancel the fire pit.|Ne Wind is the prominent wind in summer which would cancel the fire pit more often than not i reckon.|I shall not be staying there again
So great to have time with someone you love in a place you love…

Well what a beautiful park right across the road from the most amazing beach. Great service on last minute overnights check in …we were on a great site (facing north!!) on our own …not just packed in as the park was not busy with most of the weekenders having left on the Sunday. An interesting drive in on the road but well worth the effort… The guest “lounge “ outdoor area & decking overlooking the beach was also a highlight … really good clean amenities and services… cant wait to be able to go back and stay longer.
Love Seal Rocks, beautiful,  peaceful  !😍
Ran down cabins and over priced

Love the location but unfortunately we were stuck in a standard cabin that was a little outdated and small. The bathroom had the shower leaking water and had  water damage. Next time I will probably stay in a nicer cabin.

I’d give it zero stars if I could. I booked a last-minute holiday for my partner and me seven days before our check-in date, with the weather forecast looking clear at the time. I understand the cancellation and refund policy is clearly stated, but short-notice bookings often allow for some discretion, especially under exceptional circumstances.

My partner is six months pregnant and we live seven hours away. A severe and unprecedented weather system moved in just before our trip, with official warnings in place for the drive. The “BOM cyclone” was expected to impact most of the coast, including the area we were meant to travel to. Concerned, my partner contacted the property five days in advance requesting a refund or at least a partial credit, but they flat-out refused, claiming it was just “drizzling.” That week (days we would have been travelling) there were 90–100 km/h winds happening along the coast and multiple family members urged us not to go due to safety concerns. One family member even had a large tree fall on their home and called to say, “It’s lucky you didn’t go.”

After their refusal, they ignored our follow-up message entirely. I felt that offering a credit would have been a fair and reasonable compromise given the extreme conditions. It’s also worth noting that the park wasn't fully booked, even during the holidays, so rebooking the cabin seemed entirely possible and we would’ve happily returned and even paid more to stay another time. It felt like a missed opportunity for them to show goodwill, maintain a positive relationship, and potentially earn more in the future (a win win for them and lose lose for us).

I understand the importance of clear policies, but ignoring follow-up communication pending a major and dangerous weather event seems unreasonable. Very disappointing and I wouldn’t book here again.
A truly beautiful part of our wonderful country, not overcrowded. Staff were more than willing to help ,even offered to wash my clothes as their laundry was being upgraded...
Great stay at Seal Rocks Caravan park. Facilities were fantastic, very clean and nice warm showers. Great location with an amazing seating area to relax and admire the view. Would recommend 👌
The view is bloody amazing!

Loved the park, loved the location. The Jayco permanent caravan we stayed at was quite mouldy in places and smelt musky. Wouldn’t book this again unless they rectify. Would love to stay again in a cabin or camping ourself because the location is fantastic.

Seal Rocks Caravan Park is a beauty and the staff are wonderful. The only issue I have relates to the grass sites over the back (specifically site 86).
We take our van up there every year for our daughter, who stays on site 86.
It always rains every time she goes and that site absolutely fills up and carries the water where the awning is. If it rains hard, it stays like a small pond for hours and you can't even get out of the van unless you're willing to wade through water. It is a hole that needs to be filled properly. It has been the same for the last 3 years and needs attention.
As I said, the park is amazing, the location perfect and the staff are so courteous, helpful and just a pleasure to talk to - absolutely no issues there.
It would just be great to see as one of those sites over the back (especially 86) fixed properly for use.

My stay at Reflections Holiday Park was, for the most part, excellent. The amenities were top-notch, and the overall experience was very positive.

However, I did encounter one minor issue: the flagpole was noticeably wonky. While it might seem like a small detail, it was quite an eyesore and honestly, it almost detracted significantly from my enjoyment of the beautiful surroundings.

This is where the story takes a positive turn! I was very impressed when a respectable elderly gentleman staying at the park took it upon himself to correct the issue with the flagpole during my stay. His initiative and kindness truly saved the day and ensured my experience remained a positive one. It speaks volumes about the community spirit at Reflections Holiday Park.

Despite the initial flagpole issue, I would highly recommend Reflections Holiday Park. The staff were friendly, the facilities were great, and the proactive action of that kind elderly gentleman truly highlighted the positive atmosphere. I look forward to returning.

Stayed in the premium villa 04-06 April 2025, checked in 4.50pm got into the villa and there was no soap or shampoo to clean ourselves with, luckily a couple gave us a spare they had extra in their room so we could have a proper shower that night. Cooking dinner half the plates, cutlery and cups had food /drink marks and clearly not clean and my partner is anaphylactic with nuts and shellfish, instead winding down in holiday mode was spent trying to find clean cutlery and plates. Saturday morning raised my issues with the 2 girls at front desk 05/04/25 and was told just go up the road to the shop and get some… sorry we paid nearly $1000 accommodation and I have to supply my own soap and clean cutlery aswell? Apparently trying to reduce small plastic waste but have me a handful from the draw at her desk… when I asked what would you do I think it’s pretty shit can’t even wash ourselves after going to the toilet or shower and was asked not to swear and basically too bad…$1000 accomodation iv seen better and cleaner bus stops. Do I care that everything higher than 6ft is covered in that thick dust grime? No… do I care my girlfriend potentially choke to death by allergic reaction from bacteria riddled cutlery while on holidays in front of our 2 young girls… yes. REFLECTIONS SEAL ROCKS JUST BE BETTER.
Definitely will be returning.
Awesome spot. Will definitely return.

You can’t beat this location it is absolutely beautiful, however as someone who has stayed 4 or 5 times at this camp site I must say the lack of cleanliness the last 2 times visiting has put me off going again. The amenities are tired and dirty and for the steep price of nearly 100$ a night for a powered site I think we will go elsewhere next time!

We camp hear each year with friends, it is al lovely little spot with a normally gentle beach across the road and the park has good showers, camp kitchen and the like.  There is another flatter beach just down the road, or typical surfing beaches a short drive away.  You will need to take in your own food and drinks with you as there is very little in the way of shops in seal rocks.

Absolutely disappointed |Pricing is poor greed 91 for a site with one swag |Had to leave a day early due to a family emergency no refund or credit!!!|Definitely avoid the place and go to treachery campsite as it is in the sport of seal rocks |New QLD owners should have stayed there |There nice mottos the post everywhere on sit are empty words |Greed

Particular Caravan Site booked and paid for. Upon arrival told by admission staff that they had given our site to someone else because upon arrival they didn't  like the site they had booked. When asked how can they justify changing our booked and paid for site, the young staff advised that Park Management reserved the right to move and allocate camp sites as they sort fit, and customers had no choice. As a valid reason for changing Caravan site was not given, only that other guests didn't like their booked site upon arrival, the whole situation stunk of a separate financial benefit being involved.

Been coming to seal rocks for the last 40 years
Charging 89 dollars for a tent site is ridiculous
Due to a family emergency I had to cut my trip short
No refund or credit
Definitely was better with the previous owners
Not in the spirit of seal rocks
New management should have stayed in QLD

We just spent the weekend in a standard cabin. We love the Seal Rocks area, but the cabin was very run down. The main blind cord in the living room was broken and the bathroom was really old and gross.

When we arrived, there was a dead cockroach on the ground in the bathroom. We were put in a really small cabin, despite having 3 small children (one being stated as an infant requiring a cot, and there was no room in the bedrooms to put a cot). In our bedroom where the queen bed was, we could barely walk between the bed and the walls of the room. It was ridiculously tight. Internet barely works, and continued to disconnect throughout the weekend, which we didn't mind but shouldn't be advertised that there is accessible Wifi when it doesn't work.

We also were staying with friends who paid for a powered site. We booked over 6 months ago and requested to stay near each other, however this request seems to have been disregarded as we were put in opposite ends of the park.

We will return to Seal Rocks, but unlikely to stay again.

We booked a year in advance thinking
we would get a Seaview cabin  - (misleading on the website) put us at the back - do not stay in  Cabin 90 for many reasons -
Much to be desired accomodation - not fit for 6 people as 2 was a squash (we are small people), old and rundown, had to sleep in kids bunk as the main bedroom mattress was so lumpy on one side - needs replacement - no tv, wifi in and out, noisy, fly screen broken so had to keep window closed,    floors not clean.
Such a pity as the location is wonderful. Book directly to avoid being disappointed.

Great location but wouldn’t stay here again. Had to prove I was staying there every time I came back from a surf or swim. Saw the manager multiple times yelling at guests and harassing visitors about ridiculous mandatory wristbands.
It's a nice place but very expensive. Bring lots of food and drinks etc because the closest supermarket (that doesn't scalp) is 40 minutes away...

Terrible cancellation/alteration policy
We Tried to change a booking 10days out, they would not cancel or change to a later date at all. They were Not willing to accept any reason or accommodate customers issues at all.  Wasted our entire cost of the site without any reimbursement or possibility to change dates.
Very disappointed!!
They don’t respond to emails. They are incredibly rude when you call. I wouldn’t bother trying to plan a holiday here. Especially when you pay a huge premium like we are over Christmas.

Wir blieben nur eine Nacht, die wir schon vor Ankunft als relativ teuer empfanden. Die Szenerie sah aus dem Auto sehr vielversprechend aus. Sobald die Türen des Autos geöffnet waren konnten wir keine Sekunde des Aufenthalts mehr genießen. Es lag dauerhaft ein dermaßen strenger Fischgeruch in der Luft, dass wir am nächsten morgen direkt nach dem aufstehen davor flohen um auf einem Parkplatz, weit weg vom übel riechenden Strand, zu frühstücken. Das WLAN war immerhin sehr schnell.
hermoso camping, buenas instalaciones y muy natural todo. estamos en el medio de la nada, no hay supermercados ni nada para abastecerse.

- great location
- all the facilities are kept clean but generally run down and need to be replaced/renovated, showers, toilets, fridge, kettle.
- playground is only suitable for grown up children, nothing for younger than 4y.o
- internet is , slow, patchy and keeps disconnecting
- intercom code is buggy
- no shade for tent areas
- camping slab is very hard/stones. Bended half of my pegs. Haven't had that issue in any other camp sites
- location is amazing but state of facilities don't justify the high end price
-will not return

Well we paid $100 a night for a small over run camp site. Amenities needed multiple cleans a day   Not enough toilets for the amount of people there. Great place for families. Would not recommend for a quiet time.   . Then I get a sms offering $30 to stay a few more hours.  Really…. Won’t be back.

We had an incredible time away; staff are friendly and so helpful and the park was beautifully presented, tidy and had plenty of areas to cook or sit back and relax. We arrived earlier than expected and were really happy when we were able to check
In early. We stayed in a cabin, there was plenty of fresh linen, it was clean and tidy, lots of cutlery and plates etc to use but we were shocked at how minimally stocked the kitchen was - there wasn’t anything like baking paper, oil etc, not even salt and pepper! The shop up the road was able to help us out to an extent, with most grocery items overpriced compared to what you’d buy them at the store yourself for (hey, they have to make a profit too right) and even some being past before the “best before” date - but that’s not the parks fault. All in all, we had a great stay and we will return, we’ll just make sure we bring the essentials like oil etc next time

Incredible location and lovely staff. The amenities blocks were clean with good hot showers. Could be great!
Unfortunately until 11pm and early in the morning there was 30+ children screaming, running in and around our campsite and being over the top loud. I understand they are kids but it became impossible to sleep and no care from the parents who were also drinking until late. Seemed to be large groups of them I assume it’s fairly common for these grounds.
We also paid $101 for one night to camp in a swag. Pretty steep price for no sleep.
If you want any privacy or sleep either go right to the very back of the grounds or stay somewhere else.

Seal Rocks is the best place to visit and stay. The beaches and walk to the lighthouse were spectacular. Shame about the caravan park.  Dynamic pricing (read gouging), poor ammenities and general untidiness left a lot to be desired. Once of stay as reflections caravan parks will be avoided in future. Managers should of stuck with their previous careers.

Seal Rocks is a magical spot and should be added to everyone’s bucket list ✨
We stayed on a powered site and couldn’t rate the caravan park highly enough. The park managers and staff went out of their way to ensure we had a great stay. The amenities were clean and well appointed. We also loved the view and facilities in the guest lounge. We will definitely be back!
When will you stop handing out your hard earned money to these price gouging camps way over priced for a camp ground how much power an water can one use look around or by pass this camp Covid tax
Great location for anyone who loves to relax and wind down to enjoy the basics of life ...enjoy for the ones making it out here

Stunning location just next to the ocean!

All the facilities and amenities are clean and well maintained.
We arrive after office hours and didn’t have too much interaction with the staff, but the booking and arrival via phone was easy and smooth.
We did a lovely walk to the lighthouse in the morning.
I would love to come back and I encourage everyone to come!

Incredible location right across from the pristine beach - but what really impressed us was how clean the park was - the amenity blocks ( toilets / showers) & the common areas were so well equipped! We didn't do any cooking or washing up in our van because the facilities in the community kitchen were so good ! Not to mention the views from the "Chill out" area / BBQ/ kitchen area. They even had live music onsite Saturday night ! Such a good vibe - very friendly staff ... we'll definitely be back & in the meantime we'll recommend your park to everyone! 🌟🌟🌟🌟🌟
Beautiful location

Tutto perfetto ad eccezione del acqua calda in doccia, che ha smesso di funzionare il giorno prima di andare via...
Impedendo a due di noi di farsi la doccia.
This is good place

I will never stay at a Reflections Caravan Park we were ripped off - we tried to change our booking we didn’t ask for a refund just to change the date as we had a 8 month old baby in our group and there were severe weather warnings for that weekend.  We were told to “Read the Terms and Conditions” which state no changes to your booking and then they added “These Terms and Conditions are very generous” - I think not and will never stay with this organisation EVER

We are so disappointed that we had booking at the Seal Rocks Holiday Park and we tried to move our booking to another date because we have Babies and very young children and this weekend the whole of the NSW East Coast is experiencing severe weather conditions we asked to move our booking to another date.  We have been advised the Terms and Conditions do not allow this so we loose our money.   Having said that you have to pay FULL payment prior to arrival.  We did email them more than 24 hours before our arrival to see if we could postpone- not ask for a refund and now have been told NO you FORFIET all moneys paid - we are talking about 3 families so more than $500 that this Seals Rock Reflection Park has TAKEN from us and all they say is READ THE TERMS and CONDITIONS - not good enough-don't ever stay at a Reflections Park let alone the one at Seals Rocks

Seal Rocks is a beautiful location. We had a standard cabin which was comfortable but very compact. Ideal for a family with four little kids or 2-3 older kids. However, there is always the option to eat outside on the spacious verandah protected from the elements. The management team go out of their way to provide entertainment for those who want it - including live music at the camp kitchen with the magical view, early morning yoga, a Balinese food truck one evening and the very special Anzac Dawn Ceremony - which even had two horses parading the length of the beach with flag bearers. You will be in good hands at Reflections Seal Rocks xxxx

Stay 7/4/24 to 20/4/24, I have been holidaying at Seal Rocks over the Easter school holiday period in April for approx 20 years. Unfortunately this year was not a pleasant experience.The park facilities in my and numerous fellow campers I spoke to, have deteriorated to the extent I would not rate the park considering the fees currently being charged.
1. The toilet and shower blocks(particularly the old amenities block) were at most times unclean,(toilet paper on floors,faeces on toilet cubicle walls, sand and water from showers throughout)It appeared they were only cleaned once a day at varying times. With the park having a large number of camping families, maybe they should have been cleaned more regularly.Campers near us had a 12 month old baby and were unable to use the family bathroom due to the unclean state it was in.
2.Children and adults riding bikes,scooters and skate boards in the park after dark(sometimes up to 9-10pm).Previous years there were restrictions on riding these items at night and policed accordingly.
3.Camp sites since the upgrade of the park have progressively deteriorated to the extent that most sites now are just dirt and gravel, compacted to the extent that if it rains they become filled with water.
As this seems to be one of Reflections premium parks and priced accordingly one would assume it would be maintained accordingly.
I am NOT writing this review just to rubbish the park, as Seal Rocks is such a beautiful spot I feel it deserves a top class holiday camping resort and unfortunately that is not the case at present.
I did email this review direct to Seal Rock Park and Refections head office 3 weeks ago and have had no acknowledgement so decided to put it in the public domain

Cabin was a little outdated.
Very close to the beach, and sugarloaf point lighthouse.
Great place to relax and enjoy nature.

🇦🇺
This campsite is fantastic, as are the surroundings and the great viewpoints.

I can't say anything about the rooms - we had a motorhome pitch. It wasn't quite as nice (no view) but it was nice and quiet.

The sanitary facilities were very well maintained and well equipped.

I can absolutely recommend this campsite👍

🇩🇪
Dieser Campingplatz ist traumhaft, genauso wie die Umgebung und die tollen Aussichtspunkte.

Zu den Zimmern kann ich nichts sagen wir hatten einen Wohnmobilstellplatz. Dieser lag nicht ganz so schön (keine Aussicht) aber dafür schön ruhig.

Die Sanitäranlagen waren sehr gepflegt und gut ausgestattet.

Ich kann diesen Campingplatz absolut empfehlen👍
Great place to relax and unwind....steps to to the beach for swim, sun-baked or surf
Perfect location right on the beach. Good, clean amenities.
Didn't stay here but used the kiosk for an ice block. No cash,,such a shame

The cheapest site was $77 to camp in a swag. Needless to say I passed. I recommend Treachery beach campground because it was only $22.
Just because your in a remote location dosent mean you can rip people off. You should be embarrassed.

Stayed on powered site at seal rocks for 2 nights. Requires a bit of upgrading like artificial turf as majority of the park is just dirt sites so in rain turns to mud and for $70 per night we expected a lot better. Amenities also could have been clean more regularly
Picturous
Busy in school holidays. Limited internet
Looked clean when we drove past.
best location in nsw
Beautiful location in Seal Rocks. The beach was the highlight. Decent amenities. Small shop walking distance nearby for essentials. Loved being by the beach for sunset.

Can it get any better!!! From arrival to exit, the super friendly SRHP staff ensure that your stay is everything and more. We had a cabin overlooking the pristine coasline, and were able to futher enjoy the family friendly offerings located throughout the park. Can even get your favourite ice creams, it's that good. Nearby surfing and fishing spots to go with local shops and coffee van, what more is left... Everybody walking around Seal Rocks Holiday Park is happy and smiling, and you will too! :)
Exceptional place.
Prime location right next to the beautiful beach. But amenities and kitchen were dirty and too few toilets and showers for so many people. Expected a cleaner place for 98$ a night.

This one of our favourite campgrounds as we slowly bounced down Australia's east coast.  Beautiful beach, tidy, clean campground, old and new amenities and friendly front staff.  WiFi was great near the office which offset the lack of decent cell signal  They have a beach lookout area setup with chairs and tables for sundown drinks/socializing.  We saw so many whales breaching and tail/fluke slapping.  Awesome stuff!  Powered site was $68/night.

NOTE:  Either the Surfer's have an agreement with the campground or they pass the amenity block numbers on by word of mouth.  After the office closes the showers get busy from Surfer's coming in and cleaning up after a long day at the beach.  Watched some even drive up, park and walk in to the amenities, then drive away,  obviously not staying there. It's fun to watch them do this from the whale watching area.  So plan your evening showers accordingly.  If there's no agreement there needs to be a bit of enforcement somehow.
Loved it here!
Great place for a family holiday. Watching whales and the beach from the deck was great. Bathrooms are clean. Just need to watch those brush turkeys.
Great spot to go and relax.

Traveled here September 2023.

Great park in such a beautiful location surrounded by hills and forrest with the beach only a short walk. Staff and amenities were faultless. The deck/viewing area is a great spot to have an arvo beer or morning coffee while checking the surf. Not a heap for kids to do inside the park, only a playground which was really good...but be careful with the black slide as it marks clothes and doesn't wash out. Sites are a little exposed to wind so pack some long sleeves just in case.

The beach is great, surf at the point on lower tide is fun for beginner/intermediate surfers with the beachies more challenging. Lighthouse is a good trip and beach driving and surfing is good here when winds from Nth. You will need a NPWS permit.
The best most beautifully situated and set up caravan park I’ve been to. I want to move in and never leave. The facilities are amazing, the views and sounds of the ocean are heaven.

Seal Rocks is such an amazing place. Quiet, relaxing, and beautiful views. The Seal Rocks Reflections Holiday Park is in THE PERFECT location to enjoy this amazing tiny coastal town. We'll definitely be back. Loved it, especially the camp kitchen as the sun goes down...
What a beautiful location. Saw whales and dolphins and gorgeous sunsets

We have stayed at the park a few times now. Dan and Katie, the park managers are so accomodating and friendly.
Always clean & tidy and the view can’t get any better!! Love it!
We’ll be back!

Midwinter break, school holidays but not overcrowded.
Friendly, helpful staff.
Lovely beaches.

What a gem!  Fantastic location across the road from the beach with beautiful views.  Park managers ver friendly and welcoming.  We had a caravan, sites are very roomy.  Amenities nice and clean.  The outdoor BBQ/alfresco area is magnificent.  Highly recommend 😊

I honestly can't think of one single thing that I didn't like about staying here.  We stayed on a powered site with the van and at first we were put down the back but due to disability we asked to be moved closer to amnesties block which the lovely girl in the office did without hesitation.  The camp kitchen of which there are 2 was kept immaculate (as were the toilets and showers) they have tvs and foxtel in both also. They have a laundry which is only $6 for washers which is pretty cheap . The atmosphere here is so laid back . It's directly opposite the beach so at night u go to sleep with the sound of waves crashing . We stayed in a busy period but even then everything was peaceful and quiet around 8pm. Cannot wait to stay again.
Lieblingsplatz meiner Frau. Einfach herrlich hier.

Great camping spot. Able to set up star link without issues. Only shame is we couldn't have fires going - it gets very cold when the sun goes down (we came here in June) and the wind is chilly. Having a fire going would bring it up another level.

Amenities were clean and have been updated recently. Fantastic place to work on the road from.
Very very friendly and helpful front desk

We camped at a few places on our way down from Brisbane and this was by far the best. It was clean, the managers were so friendly and sat by the fire chatting to us at the end of the day and you can tell they have a lot of pride in the property. Will be coming back again for sure, magic location.

After a mix up with bookings settled down to have a wonderful week.
Accommodation rates are quite expensive when there are no local services.
